panels.description: Status of the last metric collection. OK means no errors occurred during the last collection.
panels.title: Collector Status

panels.description: Number of databases configured in PgBouncer.
panels.title: Databases

panels.description: Number of connection pools.
panels.title: Pools

panels.description: Number of users configured in PgBouncer.
panels.title: Users

panels.description: Number of free (unallocated) client connection slots. A low number here indicates the connection limit is nearly reached.
panels.title: Free Clients

panels.description: Number of currently used (active) client connections.
panels.title: Used Clients

panels.description: Number of free (unallocated) server connection slots. A low number here indicates the server pool is saturated.
panels.title: Free Servers

panels.description: Number of currently used (active) server connections.
panels.title: Used Servers

panels.description: Number of client connections currently in the login state.
panels.title: Login Clients

panels.description: Client connections that are linked to a server connection and can process queries.
panels.title: Active Client Connections

panels.description: Client connections that have sent queries but have not yet got a server connection. Consistently non-zero values indicate the pool size may need to be increased.
panels.title: Waiting Client Connections

panels.description: How long the first (oldest) client in the queue has waited, in seconds. If this starts increasing, the current pool of servers does not handle requests quickly enough. The reason may be either an overloaded server or too small a pool_size setting.
panels.title: Max Client Wait Time

panels.description: Server connections that are linked to a client.
panels.title: Active Server Connections

panels.description: Server connections that are unused and immediately usable for client queries.
panels.title: Idle Server Connections

panels.description: Server connections that have been idle for more than server_check_delay and need server_check_query before reuse (sv_used), connections currently running a server check (sv_tested), and connections in the process of logging in (sv_login).
panels.title: Used / Tested / Login Server Connections

panels.description: Average number of SQL queries per second in the last stat period, per database.
panels.title: Average Queries per Second

panels.description: Average query duration in milliseconds (converted from microseconds reported by PgBouncer).
panels.title: Average Query Duration

panels.description: Average number of SQL transactions per second in the last stat period, per database.
panels.title: Average Transactions per Second

panels.description: Average transaction duration in milliseconds (converted from microseconds). Includes time spent idle in transaction.
panels.title: Average Transaction Duration

panels.description: Average bytes received from clients (recv) and sent to clients (sent) per second, per database.
panels.title: Average Network Throughput

panels.description: Average time clients spend waiting for a server connection, in milliseconds per second (converted from microseconds).
panels.title: Average Client Wait Time

panels.description: Total number of client connections in each state across all pools. Active = processing queries, Waiting = queued for a server connection, Login = authenticating.
panels.title: Total Client Connection State

panels.description: Total number of server connections in each state across all pools. Active = linked to a client, Idle = available immediately, Used = needs server_check_query before reuse.
panels.title: Total Server Connection State

list.description: Kubernetes cluster. Allows filtering across multiple clusters.
list.label: Cluster
list.name: cluster

list.description: Kubernetes namespace where PgBouncer is deployed.
list.label: Namespace
list.name: namespace

list.description: PgBouncer database to filter on. Supports multi-select.
list.label: Database
list.name: database
